$comment: Pass 1 triage record schema + worked examples. The triage RUN writes dataset/triage.json using this shape, one record per exhibitor in exhibitors.json. Examples below are ILLUSTRATIVE demonstrations of the rubric, not final verdicts — note pre_verification:true.

record_schema.name: string — company name as resolved
record_schema.rationale: string — ONE line. For kill: disclosed-fact -> opinion.
record_schema.thesis_flags: string[] — e.g. thin-wrapper, no-moat, redliner, me-too, vapour, defensible, interesting-flawed, interop, incumbent, andy-flagged, valuation-tension
record_schema.evidence_tier: int — 1|2|3, highest tier supporting the verdict
record_schema.pre_verification: bool — true until Pass 2 confirms identity + facts
record_schema.sources: string[] — URLs (required when any T3 fact is cited)

examples.name: Harvey
examples.rationale: Category-defining agentic platform with elite-firm distribution; dissect the model, the moat claim, and the burn-vs-valuation tension.

examples.name: Luminance
examples.rationale: Interesting-but-flawed: proprietary contract model and real traction, but sits in the most crowded category on the floor — good teardown of where the moat actually is.

examples.name: DocumentDrafter
examples.rationale: Generic document-drafting tool in a 15-booth redliner field with no visible proprietary data or workflow lock-in -> in my opinion a thin wrapper unlikely to outlast the category shakeout. [pending T2/T3 confirmation]
